maml:description.maml:para: Captures a physical disk to a Full Flash Update (FFU) image.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates an ISO file from a source folder.
Uses Windows ADK oscdimg to create a standard or bootable ISO from a folder. The function validates required boot files when present and supports optional no-prompt UEFI boot media generation.

Uses efisys_noprompt.bin when available for UEFI boot media.
Optional Windows ADK root path used to resolve oscdimg.exe.

maml:description.maml:para: Converts a Windows keyboard layout value to a language/culture code.

Resolves the culture tag (for example, en-US or fr-FR) from a keyboard layout hexadecimal string such as 00000409. If no keyboard layout is provided, the function attempts to detect the current layout from Win32_Keyboard. When conversion fails, a fallback language code is returned.
Keyboard layout hexadecimal value (KLID), for example 00000409 or 00010409. The function uses the trailing 4 hex characters as the LCID.
Language code to return when keyboard layout detection or conversion fails. Default is en-US.
Returns the language code in lowercase (for example en-us).
Keyboard layout hexadecimal value (KLID), for example 00000409 or 00010409. The function uses the trailing 4 hex characters as the LCID.
Language code to return when keyboard layout detection or conversion fails. Default is en-US.
Returns the language code in lowercase (for example en-us).

maml:alert.maml:para: Author: David Segura - Recast Software 2026-07-14 - Initial help block created 2026-07-14 - Added keyboard layout to language code conversion with fallback behavior

dev:remarks.maml:para: Detects the active keyboard layout from Win32_Keyboard and returns the resolved language code.
Returns fr-FR.
Returns en-us.

maml:description.maml:para: Determines which HP TPM firmware update package is required for the current device.

Queries the TPM via WMI (win32_tpm) to identify the manufacturer and firmware version. For Infineon (IFX) TPMs, compares the firmware version against known vulnerable version ranges and returns the appropriate HP softpaq package ID. Returns 'SP87753' for firmware requiring an older update package, 'SP94937' for firmware requiring the newer package, or $false if no update is needed or the TPM is not Infineon.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires access to the root\cimv2\security\MicrosoftTPM WMI namespace. Must be run with administrator privileges.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns 'SP87753', 'SP94937', or $false.

maml:description.maml:para: Returns cached OSDCloud content found on local file system drives.
Enumerates mounted file system drives and discovers OSDCloud cache content. Returns objects with Type, Name, FullName, SizeMB, DriveRoot, VolumeLabel, and VolumeUniqueId properties.
If Type is omitted, retur ns all supported cache content types.
Type values: - ESD: All .esd files under '<DriveLetter>:\OSDCloud\OS' recursively.
- ISO: All .iso files under '<DriveLetter>:\OSDCloud\ISO' recursively.
- DriverPacks: All .cab, .exe, .msi, and .zip files under

'<DriveLetter>:\OSDCloud\DriverPacks' recursively. - Drivers: Immediate folders under '<DriveLetter>:\OSDCloud\Drivers' that contain at least one .inf file in any child folder. - Profiles: Immediate folders under '<DriveLetter>:\OSDCloud\Profiles'. - WIM: All .wim files under '<DriveLetter>:\OSDCloud\WIM' recursively.
- *: Includes all supported Type values.
Optional cache content selector.
Supports one or more values. Use '*' to return all supported cache content types.
Optional list of drive letters to include when searching for OSDCloud cache content. Accepts values such as 'C', 'D:', or 'E:\'.
When omitted, all mounted file system drive letters are considered.
Optional list of drive letters to exclude when searching for OSDCloud cache content. Accepts values such as 'C', 'D:', or 'E:\'.
Excluded drives are skipped even when they are also present in Include.
Optional cache content selector.
Supports one or more values. Use '*' to return all supported cache content types.
Optional list of drive letters to include when searching for OSDCloud cache content. Accepts values such as 'C', 'D:', or 'E:\'.
When omitted, all mounted file system drive letters are considered.
Optional list of drive letters to exclude when searching for OSDCloud cache content. Accepts values such as 'C', 'D:', or 'E:\'.
Excluded drives are skipped even when they are also present in Include.

dev:type.maml:name: System.Object[]. Objects with Type, Name, FullName, SizeMB,

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns all supported cache content types.
Returns all .esd files under each discovered cache OS folder.
Returns all .esd files and driver pack files from each discovered cache.
Returns all supported cache content types.
Searches only drive C for supported cache content types.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves disk objects suitable for OS deployment with enhanced filtering capabilities.

Get-OSDCoreDeploymentDisk queries the system for physical disks and returns disk objects with extended properties including MediaType. The function automatically filters out offline disks, disks with no media, and incompatible bus types (USB, Virtual, etc.). It provides comprehensive filtering options based on disk properties such as boot status, bus type, media type, and partition style.
Specifies the disk number to retrieve. Can also be referenced using aliases 'Disk' or 'DiskNumber'.

Filters disks by one or more specific bus types. Valid values: '1394', 'ATA', 'ATAPI', 'Fibre Channel', 'File Backed Virtual', 'iSCSI', 'MMC', 'MAX', 'Microsoft Reserved', 'NVMe', 'RAID', 'SAS', 'SATA', 'SCSI', 'SD', 'SSA', 'Storage Spaces', 'USB', 'Virtual'

Excludes disks with specified bus types. Valid values: '1394', 'ATA', 'ATAPI', 'Fibre Channel', 'File Backed Virtual', 'iSCSI', 'MMC', 'MAX', 'Microsoft Reserved', 'NVMe', 'RAID', 'SAS', 'SATA', 'SCSI', 'SD', 'SSA', 'Storage Spaces', 'USB', 'Virtual'
Filters disks by one or more specific media types. Valid values: 'SSD', 'HDD', 'SCM', 'Unspecified'
Excludes disks with specified media types. Valid values: 'SSD', 'HDD', 'SCM', 'Unspecified'
Filters disks by one or more specific partition styles. Valid values: 'GPT', 'MBR', 'RAW'
Excludes disks with specified partition styles. Valid values: 'GPT', 'MBR', 'RAW'
Filters disks where the system boots from the disk.
Filters disks that contain boot partitions.
Filters disks based on read-only status.
Filters disks that contain system partitions.
Specifies the disk number to retrieve. Can also be referenced using aliases 'Disk' or 'DiskNumber'.
Filters disks where the system boots from the disk.
Filters disks that contain boot partitions.
Filters disks based on read-only status.
Filters disks that contain system partitions.

Filters disks by one or more specific bus types. Valid values: '1394', 'ATA', 'ATAPI', 'Fibre Channel', 'File Backed Virtual', 'iSCSI', 'MMC', 'MAX', 'Microsoft Reserved', 'NVMe', 'RAID', 'SAS', 'SATA', 'SCSI', 'SD', 'SSA', 'Storage Spaces', 'USB', 'Virtual'

Excludes disks with specified bus types. Valid values: '1394', 'ATA', 'ATAPI', 'Fibre Channel', 'File Backed Virtual', 'iSCSI', 'MMC', 'MAX', 'Microsoft Reserved', 'NVMe', 'RAID', 'SAS', 'SATA', 'SCSI', 'SD', 'SSA', 'Storage Spaces', 'USB', 'Virtual'
Filters disks by one or more specific media types. Valid values: 'SSD', 'HDD', 'SCM', 'Unspecified'
Excludes disks with specified media types. Valid values: 'SSD', 'HDD', 'SCM', 'Unspecified'
Filters disks by one or more specific partition styles. Valid values: 'GPT', 'MBR', 'RAW'
Excludes disks with specified partition styles. Valid values: 'GPT', 'MBR', 'RAW'

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires .NET System.Management access to the MSFT_Disk class and the Storage module Get-PhysicalDisk cmdlet. Automatically excludes: File Backed Virtual, MAX, Microsoft Reserved, USB, and Virtual bus types. The function throws an error if no disks match the specified criteria. A warning is issued when multiple disks match the criteria.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns all available deployment-ready disks, excluding USB, virtual, and other incompatible bus types.
Returns disk 0 if it meets deployment criteria.
Returns all SSD disks suitable for deployment.
Returns all NVMe or SATA disks with GPT partition style.
Returns all non-USB, non-HDD disks (typically SSDs and NVMe drives).

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads and parses the Dell driver pack catalog for Windows 11.

Retrieves the latest Dell DriverPackCatalog.cab from Dell's download site, extracts and parses it to create a catalog of available Windows 11 driver packs. If online retrieval fails, the function falls back to the bundled local catalog.
Path to the local fallback Dell catalog XML file. This file is used when the online catalog cannot be downloaded or extracted.
URL to the online Dell DriverPack catalog CAB file.
Forces download and rebuild of the temporary online catalog even when a cached temp catalog file already exists.
Uses only local catalog values and skips online catalog download/extraction.

Uses only local catalog values and skips online catalog download/extraction.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves the Microsoft Surface driver pack catalog, enriching entries from live download pages.

Loads the bundled surface.json catalog as the offline base. For entries that include an UpdatePage URL, the function scrapes the corresponding Microsoft download page to find the newest available MSI and updates FileName, Url, and ReleaseDate accordingly. Results are cached in $env:TEMP so subsequent calls within the same session skip network requests. Falls back to base JSON values when a page cannot be reached.
Path to the local fallback Surface catalog JSON file.
Forces bypass of the temp cache and rebuilds the enriched catalog from the local Surface catalog.
Uses only local catalog values and skips connectivity probing and all live UpdatePage checks.
Path to the local fallback Surface catalog JSON file.
Forces bypass of the temp cache and rebuilds the enriched catalog from the local Surface catalog.
Uses only local catalog values and skips connectivity probing and all live UpdatePage checks.

dev:type.maml:name: Objects with CatalogVersion, ReleaseDate, Name, Manufacturer, Model, SystemId, FileName,
Url, OperatingSystem, OSArchitecture, and HashMD5 properties.

maml:alert.maml:para: Base catalog: core/driverpacks/surface.json (bundled with the module) Temp cache: $env:TEMP\osdcloud-driverpack-surface.json

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns all Surface driver pack entries, with live URLs where available.
Returns all Surface driver pack entries with verbose progress output.
Bypasses the temp cache and rebuilds the enriched catalog.
Processes only local catalog values without any live network checks.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves driver pack information for the specified manufacturer and operating system architecture.

Gets driver pack catalogs based on the device manufacturer and OS architecture. For AMD64 architecture, manufacturer-specific catalogs are loaded. For ARM64 and other architectures, the default catalog is returned. Supports Dell, HP, Lenovo, Microsoft (Surface), and generic devices.
{{ Fill GenericDriverPackJson Description }}
The device manufacturer name. Defaults to the value from $global:OSDCoreDevice.OSDManufacturer. Supported values: Dell, HP, Lenovo, Microsoft, or any other value will use the Default catalog.
The operating system architecture. Defaults to the value from $global:OSDCoreDevice.ProcessorArchitecture. Typically 'amd64' or 'arm64'.
{{ Fill GenericDriverPackJson Description }}
The device manufacturer name. Defaults to the value from $global:OSDCoreDevice.OSDManufacturer. Supported values: Dell, HP, Lenovo, Microsoft, or any other value will use the Default catalog.
The operating system architecture. Defaults to the value from $global:OSDCoreDevice.ProcessorArchitecture. Typically 'amd64' or 'arm64'.

dev:type.maml:name: Array of driver pack objects containing driver information for the specified manufacturer and architecture.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires manufacturer-specific cmdlets (Get-OSDCoreDriverPackCatalogDell, Get-OSDCoreDriverPackCatalogHP, etc.) to be available.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns driver packs for the current device's manufacturer and architecture.
Returns driver packs for Dell devices with AMD64 architecture.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves metrics for the OSD PowerShell module and OSDCloud deployment methods.

The Get-OSDMetrics script retrieves metrics for the OSD PowerShell module and OSDCloud deployment methods. It displays the latest version of the OSD PowerShell module, the date it was published, and the number of times it has been installed or saved. It also displays metrics for OSDCloud CLI, OSDCloud GUI, and OSDCloud Azure deployment methods, including the number of devices deployed, the current usage rate, and the number of devices deployed per day, week, month, and year.

maml:alert.maml:para: Author: David Segura - Recast Software 2026-07-09 - Standardized comment-based help metadata and links. 2026-07-09 - Requires OSD, OSDCloudCLI, OSDCloudGUI, and OSDCloudAzure modules.

[ValidateScript({ if (!($_ | Test-Path)) { throw 'Path does not exist' } if (!($_ | Test-Path -PathType Container)) { throw 'Path must be a directory' } if (!(Test-Path "$_\Deployment Tools")) { throw 'Path does not contain a Deployment Tools directory' } if (!(Test-Path "$_\Windows Preinstallation Environment")) { throw 'Path does not contain a Windows Preinstallation Environment directory' } return $true })]

maml:description.maml:para: Initializes the OSDCloud startnet environment.
This function initializes the OSDCloud startnet environment by performing the following tasks: - Creates a log path if it does not already exist.
- Copies OSDCloud config startup scripts to the mounted WinPE.
- Initializes a splash screen if a SPLASH.JSON file is found in OSDCloud\Config.
- Initializes hardware devices.
- Initializes wireless network (optional).
- Initializes network connections.
- Updates PowerShell modules.
Specifies whether to connect to a wireless network. If this switch is specified, the function will attempt to connect to a wireless network using the Start-WinREWiFi function.

maml:description.maml:para: Collects local hardware, firmware, TPM, and network details for OSDCloud.

Initialize-OSDCoreDevice gathers device information from CIM classes, firmware, and environment data, then normalizes manufacturer/model/product values for workflow use. It writes diagnostic logs to $env:TEMP\osdcloud-logs, attempts to copy logs to an available OSDCloudLogs path, and populates $global:OSDCoreDevice with an ordered property set used by downstream OSDCloud deployment logic.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not emit pipeline output.

maml:alert.maml:para: Side effects: - Clears the current PowerShell error collection.
- Updates date/time in WinPE when needed.
- Writes logs to $env:TEMP\osdcloud-logs.
- Sets $global:OSDCoreDevice.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Collects current device metadata, creates or updates $global:OSDCoreDevice, and writes log artifacts for troubleshooting.

maml:description.maml:para: Installs or updates the HP Client Management Script Library (HPCMSL) PowerShell module.

Ensures the HPCMSL module (version 1.8.5) is installed and up to date from the PowerShell Gallery. If PowerShellGet 2.2.5 or later is not present, it is installed first. Compares the installed HPCMSL version against the Gallery version and installs if missing or outdated. Supports both WinPE and full Windows environments. After installation, the module is imported into the global scope.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires internet access to reach the PowerShell Gallery. Must be run with administrator privileges. Uses the $WindowsPhase variable to detect WinPE vs. full OS context.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Installs or updates HPCMSL 1.8.5 for all users and imports it into the current session.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates and synchronizes an offline HPIA repository for the local HP platform.
Builds a local repository using HPCMSL commands, applies platform and OS filters, and downloads selected update content for offline use. Logs are written to C:\OSDCloud\Logs\HPIAOfflineSync.log.
Update category filter for repository content. Valid values are All, BIOS, Driver, Software, Firmware, and UWPPack.
Operating system filter passed to Add-RepositoryFilter, such as win11.
Operating system release filter passed to Add-RepositoryFilter, such as 23H2.
Update category filter for repository content. Valid values are All, BIOS, Driver, Software, Firmware, and UWPPack.
Operating system filter passed to Add-RepositoryFilter, such as win11.
Operating system release filter passed to Add-RepositoryFilter, such as 23H2.

maml:alert.maml:para: Author: David Segura - Recast Software 2026-07-13 - Initial help block created

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates an offline repository for the local platform using default Driver, win11, and 23H2 filters.
Creates an offline repository filtered to Windows 10 22H2 BIOS content.

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads and applies the HP SP94937 softpaq to downgrade a TPM from 2.0 to 1.2.

Downloads softpaq SP94937 using HPCMSL, extracts it, and runs TPMConfig64.exe with the '-a 1.2' argument to downgrade an Infineon TPM from firmware version 2.0 to 1.2. Disables Virtualization Technology (VTx) in the BIOS via Set-HPBIOSSetting before applying the firmware change.
The folder path where the softpaq EXE will be downloaded and extracted. Defaults to $env:TEMP\TPM if not specified.
The folder path where the softpaq EXE will be downloaded and extracted. Defaults to $env:TEMP\TPM if not specified.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires HPCMSL and the HP BIOS WMI interface (Set-HPBIOSSetting). Must be run with administrator privileges. A system reboot is typically required after the firmware change takes effect.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Downloads SP94937 to $env:TEMP\TPM and downgrades the Infineon TPM to spec 1.2.

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads and extracts the required HP TPM firmware update softpaq using HPCMSL.

Calls Get-HPTPMDetermine to identify the required softpaq, then uses the HPCMSL Get-Softpaq cmdlet to download it to the specified working folder. The downloaded EXE is silently extracted to a subfolder. Returns the path to the extracted folder. Intended for manual download and testing scenarios.
The folder path where the softpaq EXE will be downloaded and extracted. Defaults to $env:TEMP\TPM if not specified.
The folder path where the softpaq EXE will be downloaded and extracted. Defaults to $env:TEMP\TPM if not specified.

dev:type.maml:name: Returns the path to the extracted firmware folder.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires internet access and the HPCMSL PowerShell module. Must be run with administrator privileges.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Downloads and extracts the required TPM firmware softpaq to $env:TEMP\TPM.
Downloads and extracts the required TPM firmware softpaq to C:\Temp\TPMWork.

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads the required HP TPM firmware EXE to C:\OSDCloud\HP\TPM.

Calls Get-HPTPMDetermine to identify the required softpaq, then downloads the firmware EXE to C:\OSDCloud\HP\TPM. If the file is already present on a connected OSDCloud USB drive it is copied locally instead of being downloaded from the internet. The destination folder is cleared before each run. Also disables Virtualization Technology (VTx) in the BIOS via Set-HPBIOSSetting.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires HPCMSL if the firmware file is not already available on an OSDCloud USB drive. Must be run with administrator privileges.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Determines the required TPM softpaq and downloads (or copies) it to C:\OSDCloud\HP\TPM.

maml:description.maml:para: Extracts and installs the HP TPM firmware update from C:\OSDCloud\HP\TPM.

Locates the firmware EXE in C:\OSDCloud\HP\TPM, silently extracts it, then runs TPMConfig64.exe with the specified arguments to apply the TPM firmware update. Logs activity to C:\OSDCloud\Logs\TPMConfig.log. Outputs the exit code from TPMConfig64 along with a human-readable description for all documented exit codes.
Reserved parameter. Not currently used.
Optional firmware binary filename passed to TPMConfig64 via the -f argument.
Optional TPM specification version to target (e.g., '1.2' or '2.0'). Passed to TPMConfig64 via the -a argument.
Reserved parameter. Not currently used.
Reserved parameter. Not currently used.
Reserved parameter. Not currently used.
Optional firmware binary filename passed to TPMConfig64 via the -f argument.
Optional TPM specification version to target (e.g., '1.2' or '2.0'). Passed to TPMConfig64 via the -a argument.
Reserved parameter. Not currently used.
Reserved parameter. Not currently used.

maml:alert.maml:para: Run Invoke-HPTPMEXEDownload first to stage the firmware file. Must be run with administrator privileges. Exit code 3010 indicates success with a required reboot.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Installs the TPM firmware using default TPMConfig64 arguments.
Installs the TPM firmware targeting the 1.2 specification.

Runs Windows Setup with the quiet UI mode.

Completes previously started setup operations and immediately reboots to start the offline phase.
Enables Windows Setup Dynamic Update so setup can search for and install updates during the upgrade.

maml:description.maml:para: Executes the core OSDCloud deployment workflow.
Invoke-OSDCloud initializes runtime state in $Global:OSDCloud, merges user-provided configuration from global customization hashtables, and runs the end-to-end operating system deployment process.

The function is the main execution engine used by OSDCloud entry points such as Start-OSDCloud, Start-OSDCloudCLI, and GUI launch workflows. It discovers startup/shutdown scripts, applies automation artifacts (for example Autopilot JSON), prepares deployment resources, and orchestrates imaging and post-configuration actions.
This function accepts no direct parameters and relies on module/global state populated earlier in the launch sequence.

dev:type.maml:name: None. Pipeline input is not supported.
Primarily host/progress output and state changes in $Global:OSDCloud. The function is intended to
perform actions rather than emit structured pipeline objects.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Designed for OSDCloud automation and interactive deployment scenarios in WinPE and full Windows.
- Uses and updates global variables including $Global:OSDCloud, $Global:StartOSDCloud,
$Global:StartOSDCloudCLI, and $Global:MyOSDCloud when present. - Should be called from OSDCloud launch functions that prepare prerequisite state.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Runs OSDCloud using the current global configuration.
ZTI = $true SkipAutopilot = $true } Invoke-OSDCloud Applies custom values from $Global:MyOSDCloud and starts deployment.

maml:description.maml:para: Write the current clipboard text to the Azure clipboard Key Vault.

maml:description.maml:para: This function copies SetupComplete Files to the Local OSDCloud SetupComplete Folder Then onfigures the System SetupComplete.ps1 File to run the Custom Scripts from the OSDCloud SetupComplete Folder.

This function checks for the presence of an OSDCLoud SetupComplete Folder on any drive other than 'C'. Sorts the drives in Descending order and returns $true if the SetupComplete Folder with files inside is found. Copies the SetupComplete Files to the Local OSDCloud SetupComplete Folder. Then onfigures the System SetupComplete.ps1 File to run the Custom Scripts from the OSDCloud SetupComplete Folder.

maml:alert.maml:para: Sorting in descending order is done to try and have the USB Drive take precedence over any other drives.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the PowerShell Execution Policy of a Windows Image .wim file (Mount | Set | Dismount -Save)
Sets the PowerShell Execution Policy of a Windows Image .wim file (Mount | Set | Dismount -Save)

Specifies the new execution policy. The acceptable values for this parameter are: - Restricted. Does not load configuration files or run scripts. Restricted is the default execution policy. - AllSigned. Requires that all scripts and configuration files be signed by a trusted publisher, including scripts that you write on the local computer. - RemoteSigned. Requires that all scripts and configuration files downloaded from the Internet be signed by a trusted publisher. - Unrestricted. Loads all configuration files and runs all scripts. If you run an unsigned script that was downloaded from the Internet, you are prompted for permission before it runs. - Bypass. Nothing is blocked and there are no warnings or prompts. - Undefined. Removes the currently assigned execution policy from the current scope. This parameter will not remove an execution policy that is set in a Group Policy scope.
Specifies the location of the WIM or VHD file containing the Windows image you want to mount.
Index of the WIM to Mount Default is 1

Specifies the new execution policy. The acceptable values for this parameter are: - Restricted. Does not load configuration files or run scripts. Restricted is the default execution policy. - AllSigned. Requires that all scripts and configuration files be signed by a trusted publisher, including scripts that you write on the local computer. - RemoteSigned. Requires that all scripts and configuration files downloaded from the Internet be signed by a trusted publisher. - Unrestricted. Loads all configuration files and runs all scripts. If you run an unsigned script that was downloaded from the Internet, you are prompted for permission before it runs. - Bypass. Nothing is blocked and there are no warnings or prompts. - Undefined. Removes the currently assigned execution policy from the current scope. This parameter will not remove an execution policy that is set in a Group Policy scope.
Specifies the location of the WIM or VHD file containing the Windows image you want to mount.
Index of the WIM to Mount Default is 1

maml:alert.maml:para: 21.2.1 Initial Release

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the PowerShell Execution Policy of a mounted Windows Image
Sets the PowerShell Execution Policy of a mounted Windows Image

Specifies the new execution policy. The acceptable values for this parameter are: - Restricted. Does not load configuration files or run scripts. Restricted is the default execution policy. - AllSigned. Requires that all scripts and configuration files be signed by a trusted publisher, including scripts that you write on the local computer. - RemoteSigned. Requires that all scripts and configuration files downloaded from the Internet be signed by a trusted publisher. - Unrestricted. Loads all configuration files and runs all scripts. If you run an unsigned script that was downloaded from the Internet, you are prompted for permission before it runs. - Bypass. Nothing is blocked and there are no warnings or prompts. - Undefined. Removes the currently assigned execution policy from the current scope. This parameter will not remove an execution policy that is set in a Group Policy scope.
Specifies the full path to the root directory of the offline Windows image that you will service If a Path is not specified, all mounted Windows Images will be modified

Requires that all scripts and configuration files downloaded from the Internet be signed by a trusted publisher. - Unrestricted. Loads all configuration files and runs all scripts. If you run an unsigned script that was downloaded from the Internet, you are prompted for permission before it runs. - Bypass. Nothing is blocked and there are no warnings or prompts. - Undefined. Removes the currently assigned execution policy from the current scope. This parameter will not remove an execution policy that is set in a Group Policy scope.
Specifies the full path to the root directory of the offline Windows image that you will service If a Path is not specified, all mounted Windows Images will be modified

maml:description.maml:para: Prepare and start an OSDCloud deployment session (selects image, language, edition and other options).

Start-OSDCloud gathers system information, validates prerequisites (PowerShell version, network, presence of required utilities), and prepares a global configuration used by the OSDCloud workflow. It can select a Windows Feature Update image from local catalogs or an image URL, prompt the user for OS version/build/edition/culture when needed, and then calls Invoke-OSDCloud to run the deployment.
The function supports three parameter sets: - Default: Choose a Windows feature update by name (recommended for normal interactive use).
- Legacy: Older style parameters (OSVersion + OSBuild) for backward compatibility.
- CustomImage: Use a custom WIM/ESD image from disk or a provided URL.
(Optional) Computer manufacturer string. Automatically populated from Get-MyComputerManufacturer -Brief.
(Optional) Computer product string. Automatically populated from Get-MyComputerProduct.
Switch. When set, instructs the module to include firmware (MSC) catalog scanning.
Switch. Restart the computer after the deployment finishes.
Switch. Shutdown the computer after the deployment finishes.
Switch. Capture screenshots during OSDCloud WinPE using Start-ScreenPNGProcess. Screenshots are saved to $env:TEMP\Screenshots by default.
Switch. Skip AutoPilot enrollment tasks during the workflow.
Switch. Skip running the Office Deployment Tool (ODT) tasks.
Switch. Zero-touch install mode (ZTI). When set, disk wipes proceed automatically without prompting.
(Default parameter set) A validated OS selection string such as 'Windows 11 25H2 x64'. If omitted the function prompts interactively (unless ZTI is used which selects sensible defaults).
Edition of Windows to install (e.g. 'Enterprise', 'Pro', 'Home'). Affects edition mapping and activation type (Retail vs Volume).
Language/culture tag to install (for example 'en-us', 'fr-fr', 'zh-cn').
License type for the installation. Valid values are 'Retail' or 'Volume'.
(Optional) Computer manufacturer string. Automatically populated from Get-MyComputerManufacturer -Brief.
(Optional) Computer product string. Automatically populated from Get-MyComputerProduct.
Switch. When set, instructs the module to include firmware (MSC) catalog scanning.
Switch. Restart the computer after the deployment finishes.
Switch. Shutdown the computer after the deployment finishes.
Switch. Capture screenshots during OSDCloud WinPE using Start-ScreenPNGProcess. Screenshots are saved to $env:TEMP\Screenshots by default.
Switch. Skip AutoPilot enrollment tasks during the workflow.
Switch. Skip running the Office Deployment Tool (ODT) tasks.
Switch. Zero-touch install mode (ZTI). When set, disk wipes proceed automatically without prompting.
(Legacy parameter set) Operating system family, e.g. 'Windows 11' or 'Windows 10'.
(Legacy parameter set) Operating system build (alias: Build) such as '25H2','24H2','23H2','22H2'.
Edition of Windows to install (e.g. 'Enterprise', 'Pro', 'Home'). Affects edition mapping and activation type (Retail vs Volume).

(Optional) Computer manufacturer string. Automatically populated from Get-MyComputerManufacturer -Brief.

Switch. Restart the computer after the deployment finishes.

(CustomImage parameter set) Switch to prompt for a WIM/ESD file on removable media.
(CustomImage parameter set) URL to download a custom image if not available locally.
(CustomImage parameter set) Image index within a WIM/ESD. Default is 0.
(Optional) Computer manufacturer string. Automatically populated from Get-MyComputerManufacturer -Brief.

dev:type.maml:name: None. The function does not accept pipeline input.
This function populates the global variable $Global:StartOSDCloud (an ordered hashtable) with the
selected configuration and then invokes Invoke-OSDCloud. It does not return structured objects to the
pipeline beyond writing progress and informational messages.

maml:alert.maml:para: Author: David Segura - Recast Software 2026-07-09 - Standardized comment-based help metadata and links. - Requires the OSD module helper functions used by the workflow (Get-FeatureUpdate, Invoke-OSDCloud, Find-OSDCloudFile, Get-MyComputerManufacturer, Get-MyComputerProduct, Start-ScreenPNGProcess, etc.). - This function changes global state: $Global:StartOSDCloud and may interact with $Global:StartOSDCloudGUI.
- Intended to be run in WinPE or full Windows with administrative privileges.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Interactive: choose image and options via menus.
Non-interactive: specify OS selection and suppress autopilot.
Use a custom image URL.

maml:description.maml:para: Start an OSDCloud deployment from Azure Storage.
Runs from WinPE, installs the OSDCloudAzure dependencies, connects to Azure, discovers available OSDCloud resources, and starts the deployment workflow when an image is available.

maml:description.maml:para: Starts the Recast OSDCloud command-line deployment workflow.

Initializes device and deployment context, discovers matching operating systems, resolves driver pack metadata for the current device (or supplied overrides), validates required dependencies, and prepares global state consumed by the Recast OSDCloud CLI workflow.
Operating system architecture used when selecting catalog entries. Supported values are amd64 and arm64.
Operating system release identifier used for catalog selection.
Operating system language code used for catalog selection. If not specified, the value is inferred from the current keyboard layout.
Operating system activation channel used for catalog selection.
Operating system edition used for catalog selection. Valid values depend on OSArchitecture at runtime.
Overrides the detected computer manufacturer for driver pack matching. If omitted, the detected device manufacturer is used.
Overrides the detected computer model for logging and context alignment. If omitted, the detected device model is used.
Overrides the detected computer product/system ID for driver pack matching. If omitted, the detected device product value is used.
Operating system architecture used when selecting catalog entries. Supported values are amd64 and arm64.
Operating system release identifier used for catalog selection.
Operating system language code used for catalog selection. If not specified, the value is inferred from the current keyboard layout.
Operating system activation channel used for catalog selection.
Operating system edition used for catalog selection. Valid values depend on OSArchitecture at runtime.
Overrides the detected computer manufacturer for driver pack matching. If omitted, the detected device manufacturer is used.
Overrides the detected computer model for logging and context alignment. If omitted, the detected device model is used.
Overrides the detected computer product/system ID for driver pack matching. If omitted, the detected device product value is used.

maml:description.maml:para: Starts the Recast OSDCloud graphical deployment workflow.

Initializes device and deployment context, discovers matching operating systems, resolves driver pack metadata for the current device (or supplied overrides), validates required dependencies, and then prepares global state consumed by the Recast OSDCloud GUI workflow.
Sets the branding text shown in the OSDCloud GUI title/header. Defaults to the module resource value.
Overrides the detected computer product/system ID for driver pack matching. If omitted, the detected device product value is used.
Sets the branding color shown in the OSDCloud GUI. Provide a hex color value, for example '#0096D6'.
Operating system architecture used when selecting catalog entries. Supported values are amd64 and arm64.
Operating system release identifier used for catalog selection.
Operating system language code used for catalog selection. If not specified, the value is inferred from the current keyboard layout.
Operating system activation channel used for catalog selection.
Operating system edition used for catalog selection. Valid values depend on OSArchitecture at runtime.
Overrides the detected computer manufacturer for driver pack matching. If omitted, the detected device manufacturer is used.
Overrides the detected computer model for logging and context alignment. If omitted, the detected device model is used.
Legacy compatibility switch. This parameter is non-functional and retained temporarily to avoid breaking existing scripts.
Sets the branding text shown in the OSDCloud GUI title/header. Defaults to the module resource value.
Sets the branding color shown in the OSDCloud GUI. Provide a hex color value, for example '#0096D6'.
Operating system architecture used when selecting catalog entries. Supported values are amd64 and arm64.
Operating system release identifier used for catalog selection.
Operating system language code used for catalog selection. If not specified, the value is inferred from the current keyboard layout.
Operating system activation channel used for catalog selection.
Operating system edition used for catalog selection. Valid values depend on OSArchitecture at runtime.

Total number of screenshots to capture. Default is 9999

maml:description.maml:para: Tests whether the current HP platform is supported by HPIA.
Downloads the HP platform catalog, reads the platform IDs from the XML, and compares the local baseboard product ID to determine whether HPIA support is available on this device.

maml:alert.maml:para: Author: David Segura - Recast Software 2026-07-13 - Initial help block created

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns True when the current device platform is listed in the HPIA platform catalog.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests whether HP TPM firmware packages exist on an OSDCloud USB drive.

Searches for HP TPM firmware softpaq files (SP87753 and/or SP94937) on a connected OSDCloud USB volume. If found, optionally copies them to C:\OSDCloud\HP for local use. Returns $true if the requested package(s) are found, otherwise $false.
The HP softpaq package ID to check for. Valid values are 'SP87753' or 'SP94937'. If not specified, both packages are checked.
Switch to indicate that found firmware files should be copied to C:\OSDCloud\HP. Note: this parameter is currently unreachable due to early return statements when a PackageID is specified.
The HP softpaq package ID to check for. Valid values are 'SP87753' or 'SP94937'. If not specified, both packages are checked.
Switch to indicate that found firmware files should be copied to C:\OSDCloud\HP. Note: this parameter is currently unreachable due to early return statements when a PackageID is specified.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns $true if SP94937.exe exists on the OSDCloud USB and copies it to C:\OSDCloud\HP.
Returns $true only if both SP87753.exe and SP94937.exe exist on the OSDCloud USB.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests connectivity to Microsoft Update Catalog.

Sends an HTTP request to Microsoft Update Catalog and returns True when the endpoint is reachable with a successful or redirect status code. Uses a HEAD request first, then falls back to GET if needed.
The Microsoft Update Catalog endpoint to test.
Timeout in seconds for each HTTP request attempt.
The Microsoft Update Catalog endpoint to test.
Timeout in seconds for each HTTP request attempt.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns True when the default Microsoft Update Catalog endpoint is reachable.
Tests connectivity with a shorter timeout.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests web connectivity to a target URI using an HTTP HEAD request.
Sends an HTTP HEAD request to the specified URI and returns `$true` when the request succeeds, otherwise `$false`. If a URI is provided without a scheme, `http://` is assumed.
URI to test. Values from the pipeline are supported.
URI to test. Values from the pipeline are supported.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns `$true` when the target responds to an HTTP HEAD request.
